scientific_claim: F1 cotton hybrid showed measurable heterosis for yield and growth traits
source_reported_content: F1 from Latif x Taban had total seed cotton yield 16% higher than MPV, boll number about 20% higher than MPV, and at 40 days showed higher plant weight, shoot height, and root length than MPV
study_system: Gossypium hirsutum Latif x Taban F1
limitation: complete field design and replicate details not fully visible in provided text
source_location: Results: F1 hybrid shows significant morphological heterosis
notes: needs manual PDF check; figure/table anchor uncertain

scientific_claim: F1 transcriptomes contained a substantial non-additive expression component
source_reported_content: Most F1 genes were additive, but 697-1426 genes or 10-22% were non-additively expressed across stages; MH and SM had more upregulated than downregulated F1 MPV-DEGs
limitation: differential expression does not establish causal contribution to heterosis
source_location: Results: F1 hybrid have more actively expressed genes than its parents

scientific_claim: High-parental expression level dominance was frequent among ELD genes in the F1
source_reported_content: High-parental ELD accounted for 72% in MH, 78% in SM, and 58% in 1DPA; a subset overlapped MPV-DEGs
limitation: ELD is classified from expression profiles and not functionally perturbed
source_location: Results: Importance of high-parental expression level dominance in hybrid performance
notes: inference in source should be treated cautiously

scientific_claim: Allele-specific expression analysis indicated substantial trans-regulatory effects in F1
source_reported_content: Parental genomes were resequenced, allelic SNPs were used to distinguish parental allele contributions, and the paper reports substantial allelic expression trans-regulation
method: genome resequencing; SNP-based allelic expression; cis/trans effect classification
limitation: full statistical details require methods/supplement manual check
source_location: Results: F1 hybrid exhibit substantial allelic expression trans-regulation

scientific_claim: 24-nt siRNA clusters in the hybrid were strongly associated with TE regions and nearby regulatory regions
source_reported_content: More than 74.3% of 24-nt siRNA clusters colocalized with TEs; 2-kb flanking regions had more clusters than gene-coding regions; DE 24-nt siRNA clusters were associated with subsets of nonadditively expressed MPV-DEGs
method: small RNA-seq; siRNA cluster calling; genomic colocalization
data_type: small RNA reads; genome annotation; gene expression
limitation: colocalization and differential expression do not prove direct regulation
source_location: Results: siRNA profiling and genomic distribution

scientific_claim: TEs correlated with siRNA clusters in F1 had higher methylation and lower expression
source_reported_content: The abstract and results report that transposable elements correlated with siRNA clusters in F1 had higher methylation levels but lower expression levels
method: small RNA-seq; bisulfite sequencing; TE expression analysis
limitation: correlation among siRNA, methylation, and expression is not direct causality
source_location: Abstract; Results small RNA and methylome sections
notes: Reasonable inference: consistent with RdDM-linked TE repression

scientific_claim: Known and novel miRNAs were often non-additively regulated in F1 and linked to developmental/stress-related target predictions
source_reported_content: The paper reports 105 known miRNAs from 65 families; 83 known miRNAs from 38 families were non-additively expressed; novel miRNAs were more frequently downregulated than upregulated in F1; predicted targets included MAP kinase, UDP glycosyltransferase, expansin, GRF, GRAS TF, starch synthase 2, calcium-dependent protein kinase, and ethylene response factors
method: small RNA-seq; miRNA identification; target prediction; expression correlation
data_type: miRNA expression; predicted target mRNA expression
figure_or_table_anchor: miRNA results section; exact figure/table uncertain
confidence_level: moderate for miRNA expression; low-moderate for target regulation
limitation: targets appear prediction-based and need experimental validation
source_location: Results: MiRNAs are non-additively repressed more in the F1 hybrid

scientific_claim: The authors conclude epigenetic and expression changes can help explain cotton heterosis
source_reported_content: The conclusion states that multi-omics data provide insights into how epigenetic mechanisms and gene expression patterns can lead to heterosis in allopolyploid cotton
data_type: integrated phenotype, transcriptome, small RNA, methylome
limitation: author conclusion is broader than direct causal evidence
notes: Mark as author conclusion, not independently proven causality
